missed_changes: Dominant new AI-security thesis: AI makes pre-existing bugs easier to find, so defenders should 'massively increase use of AI to red-team yourself' / arm the defenders (2026-04-11, 2026-05-07, 2026-05-13 podcast).
Strong anti-AI-doomer stance — criticized 'doomsday marketing' and called for a 'moratorium on AI doomer content,' including a pointed critique of Anthropic (2026-05-08, 2026-05-13).
Enthusiasm for agentic coding — 'vibe coding ended, agentic engineering began,' wants agent-first dev tooling and tested multiple agent harnesses (2026-01-22, 2026-03-09, 2026-03-10, 2026-05-01).
DeFi security emphasis: rate-limited minting/withdrawals, time delays for admin actions, and 'more decentralization, not less' as the fix for the recent hack wave (2026-03-25, 2026-05-13).
Passkey portability / self-custody critique — refuses to use passkeys for anything holding real value due to lack of exportability (2026-03-20 thread).
Positive framing of prediction markets, onchain lending, and RWA/tokenization as proven growth areas in the Fund 5 piece — beyond the sectors the prediction listed (2026-05-05).
AGI economics thesis: cost of automation falling faster than cost of verification, so human verification (aided by cryptography) becomes the scarce resource (2026-02-24).
Co-launched a16z Crypto Fund 5 ($2.2B) — concrete action operationalizing the bullish-on-building stance (2026-05-05).
notes: Window spans Jan–Jun 2026. Six of seven sectors-excited were reaffirmed, often via concrete action (Jolt/Layer Zero's 'Zero', World/AgentKit, x402, Zcash/ZSAs, Fund 5); network-token design was his single most-repeated theme. Among concerns only memecoins was reaffirmed (2026-05-02 'things have really changed'); leverage, mislabeled company tokens, and vanity metrics went unaddressed. UNI is marked confirmed at stance level: he repeatedly championed the exact closed-loop buyback-burn + mint-for-growth + DUNA mechanism (2026-02-08, 2026-04-23/27), though without naming the ticker. ETH and BTC token-specific value-accrual stances were absent — the window's burn discussion was about active/designed DAO mint+burn (the UNI model), not ETH's automatic EIP-1559 burn, and BTC/digital-gold never came up. Onchain games is absent: the only games comment (2026-04-24) was about AI-made games, not onchain/trustless game state. The leverage concern is absent rather than contradicted — the Fund 5 piece frames perps positively for 'price discovery,' consistent with 'fine as products,' but does not address the leverage wariness either way.
